summarization.a: The figure displays an XPS chart of the O 1s energy region of MoOx with the results of three samples superimposed.

data_extraction.a:
| Sample | Binding energy (eV) |
|---|---|
| 2 second O2 | Peaks at 530.2 and 531.8 |
| 2 second O2 & Ar | Peaks at 530.2 and 531.8 |
| 1 second O2 | Peaks at 530.2 and 531.2 |

a.question: At what binding energy does the main O 1s peak appear in all measurements?
Which oxygen-containing peak becomes stronger when the O₂ plasma step is increased from 1 second to 2 seconds?
a.answer: The one at 531.3 eV, which also shifts to 531.8 eV.

a.question: What binding energy is reported for the secondary oxygen peak in the 1-second O₂ process?
What O/Mo stoichiometry (x value in MoOx) was estimated for the 1-second O₂ plasma sample?
